question_text: Differentiate between wired and wireless transmission.
answer_text: Wired: physical link made of wire/cable through which data travels as signals such as metallic cable or fiber-optic. Wireless: data travels in air as electromagnetic waves using antenna such as bluetooth microwaves infrared radio waves.

question_text: Differentiate between URL and domain name with appropriate example.
answer_text: URL is complete internet address of a webpage. Domain name is name of organisation with top-level internet domains. Example: URL: https://www.ncert.nic.in/textbook/textbook.htm; Domain Name: ncert.nic.in

Explain the concept of Alternate Key in RDBMS with appropriate example.
answer_text: Alternate Keys are all Candidate Keys of a table not used as Primary Key. Example: If table has both RegNo and AadhaarNo as unique columns and RegNo is chosen as Primary Key then AadhaarNo becomes the Alternate Key.

Differentiate between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL with appropriate example.
answer_text: CHAR is fixed length character type: CHAR(10) always reserves 10 character spaces padding with spaces if shorter. VARCHAR is variable-length: VARCHAR(30) allows max 30 but only uses actual storage needed. Example: 'CITY' in CHAR(20) uses 20 bytes but in VARCHAR(20) uses only 5 bytes.

question_text: Write function count_Dwords() to count words ending with a digit in text file 'Details.txt' and display the count.
answer_text: def count_Dwords(): with open('Details.txt','r') as F: S=F.read(); Wlist=S.split(); count=0; for W in Wlist: if W[-1].isdigit(): count+=1; print('Number of words ending with a digit are',count)

Write function EOReplace() in Python which accepts list L of numbers and increments even numbers by 1 and decrements odd numbers by 1. E.g. L=[10,20,30,40,35,55] becomes L=[11,21,31,41,34,54]
answer_text: def EOReplace(L): for i in range(len(L)): if L[i]%2==0: L[i]=L[i]+1 else: L[i]=L[i]-1; print(L)

question_text: Write function Push(Vehicle) where Vehicle is a dictionary {Car_Name:Maker}. Push names of cars manufactured by 'TATA' (all cases) onto the stack.

Write one difference between CSV and text files. Write COURIER_ADD() to add records with fields cid s_name Source destination to courier.csv. Write COURIER_SEARCH() to take destination as input and display all courier records going to that destination.
answer_text: CSV files can be viewed in spreadsheets and require import csv module. Text files can be viewed in text editor and require no specific module. import csv; def COURIER_ADD(): f1=open('courier.csv','a',newline=''); writ=csv.writer(f1); cid=int(input('Courier id')); s_name=input('Sender'); Source=input('Source'); destination=input('Destination'); writ.writerow([cid,s_name,Source,destination]); f1.close(); def COURIER_SEARCH(): f1=open('courier.csv','r'); detail=csv.reader(f1); name=input('Destination'); [print(i) for i in detail if i[3]==name]

question_text: Why is it important to close a file before exiting? Write Add_Book() to add book records with fields book_ID B_name pub to Book.csv. Write Search_Book() to take publisher name as input and count and display number of books published by them.
answer_text: Important to close file because Python ensures any unwritten/unsaved data is flushed to the file before closing. import csv; def Add_Book(): f1=open('Book.csv','a',newline=''); writ=csv.writer(f1); book_ID=int(input('Book id')); B_name=input('Book Name'); pub=input('Publisher'); writ.writerow([book_ID,B_name,pub]); f1.close(); def Search_Book(): f1=open('Book.csv','r'); detail=csv.reader(f1); name=input('Publisher Name'); pub_count=0; [pub_count+1 for i in detail if i[2]==name]; print('NUMBER OF BOOKS:',sum(1 for i in list(csv.reader(open('Book.csv'))) if i[2]==name))
